name: neo4j-agent-memory
maintainer: Neo4j Labs (community-supported, not official Neo4j product)
last_major_release: v0.1.0 (April 3, 2026) — first public release; development status: Beta
activity: Very new (Apr 2026 initial release), ~153 GitHub stars at launch, 22 open issues, 320+ commits pre-release, active Neo4j staff authorship (William Lyon et al.). Trajectory: rapidly growing alongside create-context-graph ecosystem.
storage_backend: Neo4j (required, no alternative backends). Supports Neo4j Desktop, Docker, and Neo4j AuraDB (managed cloud). Vector index, fulltext index, and GDS (Graph Data Science) projections all rely on Neo4j features.
representation_primitive: Three-tier memory using distinct node/relationship patterns in Neo4j: (1) Short-term: (:Message) nodes linked by [:NEXT] chains per session, each message stores role, content, timestamp, session_id, and vector embedding. (2) Long-term: (:Entity) nodes typed with POLE+O labels (Person, Organization, Location, Event, Object) plus subtypes as Neo4j labels; relationships between entities are typed (e.g., :WORKS_AT, :FOUNDED_BY, :LIVES_IN, :LOCATED_IN, :MEMBER_OF, :PARTICIPATED_IN, :KNOWS). Entities have properties: name, entity_type, subtype, description, embedding, geolocation (Point). User preferences stored as (:Preference) nodes linked to entities. Facts stored as subject-predicate-object triples. (3) Reasoning: (:ReasoningTrace) → [:HAS_STEP] → (:ReasoningStep) chains, with provenance links back to entities and messages that informed each decision.
api_shape: Python library (pip install neo4j-agent-memory). Primary interface: async MemoryClient class. First-party MCP server included (stdio and SSE transport); core profile: 6 tools; extended profile (default): 16 tools. Framework integration adapters for LangChain, Pydantic AI, Google ADK, CrewAI, LlamaIndex, OpenAI Agents, Microsoft Agent Framework, Strands.
hosting: Self-host (library + Neo4j instance). Neo4j AuraDB free tier supported as the Neo4j backend. No managed hosting of neo4j-agent-memory itself — it is a library that runs inside the agent application. Neo4j AuraDB provides managed graph DB with a free tier.
llm_dependency: Ingest: configurable pipeline — spaCy (local NLP, free), GLiNER2 (zero-shot transformer NER, local), GLiREL (relation extraction, local), and/or LLM extractors (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.). With spaCy/GLiNER only, no external LLM calls at ingest. With LLM extractor enabled, one API call per conversation turn. Background enrichment via Wikipedia (free, network call) and Diffbot (commercial API, optional). Retrieval: LLM-free by default; all search modes run against Neo4j indexes without LLM calls.
schema_extensibility: Yaml-config + fixed POLE+O base. The POLE+O entity model is fixed as the base schema. Eight pre-built domain schemas (podcast, news, medical, legal, etc.) extend the base with domain-specific subtypes and relationship types. Custom domains can be added via configuration. Entity subtypes are converted to Neo4j node labels at write time. Schema evolution within a domain is flexible (add subtype labels), but the core POLE+O model is not replaceable.
schema_richness: Core entity types: 5 POLE+O top-level types (Person, Organization, Location, Event, Object) with arbitrary subtypes as Neo4j multi-labels. Additional node types: Message, Session, Preference, Fact (subject-predicate-object), ReasoningTrace, ReasoningStep. Core relationship types: NEXT (message chain), WORKS_AT, FOUNDED_BY, LIVES_IN, LOCATED_IN, MEMBER_OF, SUBSIDIARY_OF, PARTICIPATED_IN, KNOWS, HAS_STEP (reasoning). Domain schemas add domain-specific edge types. Multi-label: yes (entity_type + subtype both become labels). Multi-graph: logical separation via session_id and agent_id, not database-per-project. Total built-in entity node labels: ~7+ base + unlimited subtypes. Well below Gimle §5's 20+ entities target in built-ins, but extensible. No code-domain entity types (File, Symbol, PR, Decision) natively.
temporal_model: Snapshot with timestamps. Messages and entities carry created_at timestamps. No bi-temporal validity window on relationships — relationships record when they were created but not when they became invalid. Entity deduplication merges duplicates rather than maintaining history. Temporal queries are limited to recency filtering, not validity-window-style 'what was true at time T' queries. Significantly weaker than Graphiti's bi-temporal model.
forgetting_policy: Semantic deduplication on ingest: three-threshold deduplication strategy using both embedding similarity and fuzzy string matching (RapidFuzz) to detect and merge duplicate entities. No TTL/LRU/LFU eviction. No automatic garbage collection. Agent-driven deletion available through API. Short-term messages are ephemeral by session design but not auto-deleted from Neo4j.
ingest_latency_class: Real-time incremental (sub-second per message with spaCy/GLiNER pipeline; adds ~100-500ms for embedding generation). With LLM extractor enabled, becomes seconds-per-turn. Background enrichment (Wikipedia/Diffbot) runs asynchronously and does not block the ingest hot path. Streaming extraction supported for 100K+ token documents.
retrieval_modes: Hybrid: (1) Vector search (semantic similarity on embeddings), (2) Fulltext/BM25 (keyword matching via Neo4j fulltext index), (3) Graph traversal (custom Cypher queries to enrich results with related entities), (4) Spatial/geospatial search (radius and bounding box for Location entities with Neo4j Point properties). The memory_get_context MCP tool assembles unified context from all three memory tiers in a single call. Cross-memory-tier retrieval is a distinguishing feature.
retrieval_agenticity: Static-lookup with unified context assembly. The get_context() method retrieves from all three tiers simultaneously and returns assembled context. No built-in Self-RAG, CRAG, or multi-hop reasoning loop. Custom Cypher queries can implement arbitrary graph traversal logic. ReasoningTrace retrieval enables 'what did the agent do last time on a similar task' pattern.
retrieval_budget_control: No explicit budget control parameters (max tokens, max hops, max edges) exposed as first-class API. Result count limits available. [uncertain]
multi_project_namespace: Logical separation via agent_id and session_id on nodes — not a formal project-level namespace. Multiple agents can share one Neo4j database with isolation by agent_id. Cross-agent queries possible via direct Cypher. No database-per-project architecture. Less formal than Graphiti's group_id system.
multi_user_access: Per-agent namespace via agent_id parameter on MemoryClient. No RBAC. Multi-agent teams supported — agents can share long-term memory (same agent_id) or be isolated (different agent_ids). Reasoning memory is per-trace with provenance links. Blog post 'When Your Agents Share a Brain' demonstrates multi-agent shared memory pattern.
embedder_options: Default: sentence-transformers (local, no API key required, all-MiniLM-L6-v2 or similar). Auto-upgrades to OpenAI embeddings if OPENAI_API_KEY is set. Install extras: pip install neo4j-agent-memory[openai] for OpenAI, pip install neo4j-agent-memory[sentence-transformers] for local. Custom embedder interface available. [uncertain about exact dimension defaults]
llm_client_options: spaCy/GLiNER2/GLiREL pipeline runs fully locally with no external LLM. Optional LLM extraction layer supports OpenAI (install with [openai] extra) and Anthropic. Install extras: pip install neo4j-agent-memory[openai]. LLM can be disabled entirely (use spaCy+GLiNER only for extraction) — this is a meaningful differentiator from Graphiti for cost/privacy-sensitive applications.
cost_privacy: With spaCy+GLiNER pipeline only: no data leaves the host, near-zero API cost, ~100-200ms ingest latency. With LLM extractor (OpenAI): one API call per conversation turn (~$0.001-0.003/turn with gpt-4o-mini), conversation text sent to OpenAI. With Diffbot enrichment: entity data sent to Diffbot API (commercial, requires key). Wikipedia enrichment: entity names sent to Wikipedia API (public, no key). Default config is privacy-preserving (local models only).
code_artifact_linkage: None natively. POLE+O model has no code-specific entity types. No File, Symbol, PR, Issue, or Commit node types. Code artifacts would need to be modeled as Object subtypes with custom properties. No issue-to-symbol chain or diff-awareness.
code_analysis_features: None first-party. No call graph, diff awareness, symbol index, import resolution, or type analysis. SaaS connectors (GitHub) exist in create-context-graph (the scaffolder that uses this library), but at the neo4j-agent-memory library level, no code analysis features are provided.
memory_os_layer: Plaintext (external knowledge store). All three memory tiers (short-term, long-term, reasoning) are external graph-based stores retrieved on demand. No parametric or activation memory layer. No cross-type migration.
self_improvement_mechanism: Reasoning memory provides partial self-improvement capability: (:ReasoningTrace) records past task-solving approaches, and the agent can retrieve similar past traces to inform new decisions — a manual/implicit form of retrieval-augmented self-improvement. No automatic RL policy or LLM-reflection-based graph update. Entity deduplication and merging constitutes passive cleanup. No formal self-improvement loop.
provenance_standard: Custom internal: every node carries created_at, agent_id, session_id. ReasoningTrace nodes link to the specific messages and entities that informed each reasoning step — this constitutes a limited provenance chain. No W3C PROV, OpenTelemetry, or MCP-native trace emission. No structured provenance export format.
benchmark_results: No published benchmark results as of Apr 2026 (library released Apr 3, 2026). No LongMemEval, LoCoMo, SWE-bench, or other third-party evaluations found. Experimental/beta status. [uncertain]
strengths: 1. Local-first, privacy-preserving extraction: spaCy+GLiNER2+GLiREL pipeline requires no external LLM API calls at ingest, making it cost-free and privacy-safe by default — ideal for Gimle's on-premises deployment. 2. Three-tier unified memory with single context call: the reasoning memory tier (decision traces with full provenance) is a native first-class citizen, not bolted on — directly relevant to Gimle's need to track why architectural decisions were made. 3. Deep Neo4j integration: native Neo4j Point types for geospatial, GDS projections for graph analytics, fulltext+vector hybrid search — no impedance mismatch when running alongside palace-mcp's existing Neo4j graph.
weaknesses: 1. Weak temporal model: no bi-temporal validity windows on relationships — cannot answer 'what was the architecture at commit X' type queries, which is central to Gimle §5's bi-temporal axis requirement. 2. Very early stage (v0.1.0, Apr 2026): experimental, no SLA, no backwards-compatibility guarantees, no published benchmarks — high integration risk for a foundation dependency. 3. POLE+O schema is general-purpose: no code-domain entity types (File, Symbol, Commit, PR, Decision) and the fixed base schema adds friction when modeling software development artifacts that don't map cleanly to Person/Organization/Location/Event/Object.
fit_with_gimle_section_5: Verdict: steal-specific-ideas — three-tier memory architecture and NLP extraction pipeline are valuable patterns; do not adopt as a direct dependency due to weak temporal model and v0.1.0 instability. Ideas to steal: (1) Three-tier memory stratification: adopt short-term (session messages) / long-term (entity KG) / reasoning (decision traces) as explicit tiers in Gimle's schema. The (:ReasoningTrace) → [:HAS_STEP] → (:ReasoningStep) pattern maps directly to Gimle's need to record agent reasoning about code decisions. (2) Local NLP extraction option: plan for GLiNER2/spaCy as a fallback entity extractor for Gimle's code-event pipeline when OpenAI cost is a concern (e.g., bulk historical ingest). (3) Geospatial Point properties as inspiration for typed properties: Neo4j native Point type pattern shows value of using DB-native property types rather than stringifying everything. (4) MCP server dual-profile pattern: core (6 tools) vs extended (16 tools) profile design for the Gimle MCP server is a clean API surface pattern to consider. Schema pattern to steal (reasoning memory): `(:ReasoningTrace {task_description, agent_id, created_at, outcome})-[:HAS_STEP]->(:ReasoningStep {step_number, thought, tool_called, result, created_at})-[:INFORMED_BY]->(:Entity)` — adapt for Gimle: replace agent_id with group_id, add code_artifact links. Do NOT adopt: the POLE+O-only entity model as Gimle's primary schema (too general); the v0.1.0 library as a dependency (stability risk); the snapshot-only temporal model.
